_comment: Quick Suite MCP Actions Integration configuration template for the Model Router.
_instructions: 1. Run scripts/post-deploy.sh to retrieve your stack output values.
2. Replace every <PLACEHOLDER> with the value from that output.
3. In Quick Suite, go to Settings > Integrations > MCP Actions > New Integration.
4. Paste the filled-in values into the corresponding fields.
5. See docs/quicksuite-integration.md for a complete walkthrough with screenshots.
integration_description: Multi-provider LLM routing with Bedrock Guardrails governance. Routes analyze, generate, research, summarize, code, and extract tasks to the best available model (Bedrock, Anthropic, OpenAI, or Gemini). Supports per-department provider overrides, capability routing, and PHI-safe routing.

agentcore_gateway.comment: Register the API Gateway endpoint as an AgentCore Gateway OpenAPI target, then point Quick Suite at your AgentCore Gateway MCP endpoint.

tools.name: analyze
tools.description: Analyze data, text, or documents with the best available LLM. Use for structured analysis, comparison, evaluation, or interpretation tasks.

inputSchema.prompt: string (required) — the text or question to analyze
inputSchema.department: string (optional) — department name for provider routing override (e.g. 'openai-only', 'bedrock-preferred')
inputSchema.provider: string (optional) — force a specific provider (bedrock, anthropic, openai, gemini)

example_prompts: Analyze the sentiment trends in this survey data
Compare these two research papers and summarize the key differences
Evaluate the statistical significance of these results

tools.name: generate
tools.description: Generate content — reports, drafts, emails, presentations — with the best available LLM.

inputSchema.department: string (optional) — department name for provider routing override
inputSchema.context: string (optional) — background material to draw from

example_prompts: Draft a methods section for this research paper
Generate a grant narrative from these bullet points
Write an executive summary of this dataset analysis

example_payload.prompt: Write an executive summary of enrollment trends

tools.name: research
tools.description: Research a topic with deep reasoning capabilities. Best for multi-step investigation, literature synthesis, or hypothesis exploration.

inputSchema.department: string (optional) — department name for provider routing override

example_prompts: What are the current best practices for RNA-seq normalization?
Summarize recent advances in transformer architectures for genomics
What statistical methods are appropriate for this study design?

example_payload.prompt: What predictive factors correlate with first-year student retention?

tools.name: summarize
tools.description: Summarize documents, datasets, or text with the best available LLM. Optimized for speed and conciseness.

inputSchema.prompt: string (required) — text or document to summarize
inputSchema.department: string (optional) — department name for provider routing override

example_prompts: Summarize this 50-page technical report in 3 paragraphs
Create a bullet-point summary of these meeting notes
What are the key findings from this dataset description?

example_payload.prompt: Summarize the attached IRB protocol in plain language

tools.name: code
tools.description: Generate, review, debug, or explain code with the best available LLM. Supports Python, R, SQL, and most languages.

inputSchema.department: string (optional) — department name for provider routing override
inputSchema.context: string (optional) — existing code or data schema

example_prompts: Write a Python script to parse this CSV and compute summary statistics
Review this R code for statistical errors
Explain what this SQL query does and suggest optimizations

example_payload.prompt: Write an R script to run a logistic regression on this enrollment dataset

tools.name: extract
tools.description: Extract structured data from scientific or analytical text. Supports extraction types: effect_sizes, confounds, methods_profile, open_problems, citations. Providers use native JSON mode for reliable structured output.

inputSchema.prompt: string (required) — the text to extract from
inputSchema.extraction_types: array of strings (required) — types to extract: effect_sizes, confounds, methods_profile, open_problems, citations
inputSchema.department: string (optional) — department name for provider routing override
inputSchema.store_at_uri: string (optional) — S3 URI to persist extracted data (e.g. open_problems gap list for clAWS watch consumption)

example_prompts: Extract effect sizes and sample sizes from this clinical trial abstract
Pull the methods profile and confounders from this paper
Extract open problems and research gaps from this review article

example_payload.prompt: Treatment group showed significant improvement (OR = 1.43, 95% CI [1.12, 1.82], p = 0.004, n = 847)...

provider_stack_outputs.comment: These keys match CloudFormation output names from cdk deploy. Run scripts/post-deploy.sh to see all values.

department_overrides_example.comment: Add department_overrides to your routing_config.yaml to enable per-department routing. The 'department' field in the request payload selects the override.
